commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can preserve the existing stop-plus-visible-text guard while adding the missing silent stop-plus-tool-calls path.; Needs attention: The diff can promote a previously dropped spurious provider tool-call block into executable agent tool use, so the tool-execution boundary needs a narrower guard before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Keep stop-finished text replies from executing tools — s... (sha=8caa4cdcf51c8ba623f78e23ad869e69a66e1089)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Chat Completions stream finalizer guard that promotes `finish_reason: stop` to `toolUse` when structured tool-call blocks were assembled, plus a mocked regression test for that stream shape.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +70. Total +73 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main maps `finish_reason: stop` to `stop` and then drops tool-call blocks, matching the linked silent stop-plus-tool-call report. The PR-introduced regression is also source-reproducible because the new guard would make the existing stop-plus-visible-text spurious-tool-call test fail.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can preserve the existing stop-plus-visible-text guard while adding the missing silent stop-plus-tool-calls path.

target.title: fix(openai): honor streamed tool calls with stop finish
target.body:
Fixes #88791.

## Summary

- preserve structured Chat Completions tool-call blocks when the provider reports `finish_reason: stop`
- promote that terminal stop reason to `toolUse` only when finalized tool-call blocks are present
- keep the existing opposite guard for `tool_calls` finish reasons with no tool calls

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A streamed OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions response can contain structured `tool_calls` but finish with `finish_reason: stop`. Before this patch OpenClaw removed those tool-call blocks because the terminal reason was not `toolUse`, leaving the turn with no visible answer and no executed tool call.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/andy/openclaw-88791`, branch `fix/openai-stop-tool-calls-88791`, commit `8caa4cdcf5`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -- -t "promotes stop completions with structured tool calls|resets stopReason to stop"
git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: The targeted stream regression passed and the outgoing commit author was verified:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  2 passed | 215 skipped (217)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.40s
8caa4cdcf5 Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> fix(openai): honor streamed tool calls with stop finish
```

Observed result after fix: The mocked streamed completion with `tool_calls` plus final `finish_reason: stop` now ends with `stopReason: "toolUse"` and retains the parsed `toolCall` block so the runtime can execute it.

What was not tested: I did not run against the reporter's live vLLM/Qwen deployment; the regression uses a source-level mocked Chat Completions stream matching the reported provider shape.
